title: Dark Navy Section Layout — Design Spec
body:
# Dark Navy Section Layout — Design Spec

## Problem
The current homepage is all light-on-white (`#fafaf9` bg, `#4b5563` body text). Every section has identical visual weight — no hierarchy, no contrast anchors, reads flat.

## Source Palette (from share cards)
| Token | Hex | Use |
|-------|-----|-----|
| `navy-deep` | `#1a1a2e` | Section backgrounds (darkest, near-black navy) |
| `navy-mid` | `#16213e` | Card backgrounds on dark sections |
| `navy-bright` | `#0f3460` | Gradient end stop, accent surfaces |

Gradient shorthand: `linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e 0%, #16213e 50%, #0f3460 100%)`

## Which Sections Go Dark

| Section | Class | Treatment | Rationale |
|---------|-------|-----------|----------|
| **Hero** | `.hero` | Full dark gradient | Highest-impact, first impression; creates premium above-fold anchor |
| **Comparison** | `.comparison-section` | Solid `#16213e` + subtle grid texture | Conversion section — dark bg signals premium tier, paid card pops |
| **How It Works** | `.how-it-works` | Stay light (`#fafaf9`) | Process steps need clarity; dark would compete with hero above |
| **Framework Cards** | `.framework-cards-section` | Stay light, cards keep white bg | Content-dense; light treatment improves scannability |

Rhythm: **dark → light → light → dark** — two dark anchors bookend the content flow.

## Text Colors on Dark Backgrounds

| Role | Value | Usage |
|------|-------|-------|
| Primary heading | `#f3f4f6` | `h1`, `h2` on dark sections |
| Body / supporting text | `rgba(255,255,255,0.72)` | Paragraphs, subtitles |
| Muted / metadata | `rgba(255,255,255,0.45)` | Labels, secondary copy |
| **Amber accent** | `#f59e0b` | CTA buttons, highlight text — unchanged from light theme |
| Purple (on dark) | `#a78bfa` | Framework type chips, decorative (lighter than `#6366f1` which washes out on dark) |
| Border / divider | `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` | Card borders, horizontal rules |

## Hero Dark Treatment Detail

```css
.hero {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #1a1a2e 0%, #16213e 50%, #0f3460 100%);
  color: #f3f4f6;
  padding: 80px 24px 96px;
}
.hero-headline { color: #f3f4f6; }
.hero-subtitle  { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.72); }
/* Primary CTA: amber fill, same as existing btn-start */
/* Secondary CTA: white text, transparent bg, white underline */
.hero-cta-secondary { color: rgba(255,255,255,0.85); text-decoration: underline; }
```

## Comparison Section Dark Treatment Detail

```css
.comparison-section {
  background: #16213e;
  color: #f3f4f6;
}
.comparison-section .comparison-tier { color: #a78bfa; }
.comparison-section .comparison-card { background: rgba(255,255,255,0.05); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.10); }
/* Paid card: amber left-border accent */
.comparison-section .comparison-card.paid { border-left: 3px solid #f59e0b; }
.comparison-section li::before { color: #f59e0b; } /* checkmarks stay amber */
```

## What NOT to Change
- `.how-it-works` and `.framework-cards-section` stay on `--bg: #fafaf9`
- No change to the amber/purple color scheme — only backgrounds and text adaptations
- Dark mode (`prefers-color-scheme: dark`) already uses `#111827`/`#1f2937`; these new navy sections are **always dark** regardless of system setting

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hero section uses navy gradient, all text passes WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1 minimum)
- [ ] Comparison section uses `#16213e` background with adapted text/card colors
- [ ] Page visual rhythm: dark hero → light how-it-works → light frameworks → dark comparison
- [ ] Amber CTA buttons remain visually consistent across light and dark sections
- [ ] No text below 4.5:1 contrast ratio on any dark section (test with browser DevTools or Figma)
